title: 1981-04-16_Pat_128.txt
key_claims: The system of reality is in absolute control and actively utilizes the disbelief of humanity. True moral action can only occur when people doubt the system; if they knew of the guaranteed reward (salvation), action would not be free or moral.
Dick aligns his theological discoveries precisely with the Protestant theology of Paul and Martin Luther, updated through the 20th-century existentialism of Paul Tillich.

Salvation requires man to take the primary, existential step: he must make a free-will choice to call out to God. This call is predicated on the absolute realization of his own total powerlessness and worthlessness.
"Faith" is not a mere belief in doctrine; it is an active, existential cry for help based on an ontological crisis. Only when man makes this existential choice does God respond with Justification.
recurring_concepts: **The Paraclete**: God responding to man's existential cry for help by sending the Holy Spirit to act as advocate, comforter, and savior.
reading_excerpt: The system of reality is in absolute control and actively utilizes the disbelief of humanity. True moral action can only occur when people doubt the system; if they knew of the guaranteed reward (salvation), action would not be free or moral.
